title: Matbench Materials Property Benchmark Datasets

data_urls.access_notes: Use the benchmark website and package for official task definitions.
Matbench tasks are available through the materials ML Python ecosystem.

instructions: Use the official Matbench or matminer loaders for a selected task.
Pin the task name, benchmark version, and official train/test folds before constructing extra SPECTRA splits.
Retain formula, structure, composition, property, and fold identifiers.

recommended_axes.name: composition_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Elemental composition, Magpie-feature, or formula-vector similarity to training materials.

recommended_axes.name: structure_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Crystal-structure, prototype, space-group, or local-environment similarity.

recommended_axes.name: property_support
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from training target-property support; diagnostic unless based only on training labels for prospective construction.

useful_for: Materials-science SPECTRA demos that are smaller than large simulation corpora.
Testing composition novelty versus structure novelty.
Building non-biology examples for scientific generalization auditing.
leakage_risks: Formula duplicates and polymorphs can leak across folds.
Property-derived axes are post-hoc unless constructed from training-only labels.
Composition-only models and structure-aware models need separate axis interpretation.
